items.title: MDS Health : Une Malienne fait rayonner l’IA médicale africaine
items.description: © Internet Le Mali continue de révéler des talents capables de contribuer à la transformation numérique du continent. Parmi eux figure Maimouna Dicko, data scientist malienne, qui s’est distinguée grâce à MDS Health, un projet d’Intelligence Artificielle appliquée à la santé présenté lors du Transform Africa Summit 2025 à Conakry, en Guinée. MDS Health est présentée comme une innovation développée dans le cadre des travaux académiques de Maimouna Dicko, avec une vocation concrète pour le secteur médical. L’objectif de cette solution est de répondre à un défi majeur auquel font face de nombreux systèmes de santé africains : le manque de radiologues. Grâce à l’intelligence Artificielle, MDS Health assiste les professionnels de santé dans l’analyse des radiographies thoraciques afin d’améliorer la rapidité et la précision du diagnostic. L’outil ne remplace pas le médecin, mais constitue une aide à la décision clinique. Selon les informations rendues publiques par Cluster Digital Africa, la plateforme repose sur quatre modèles d’intelligence artificielle dédiés à la segmentation, à la détection, à la classification et à l’analyse multi-labels des images médicales. Les premiers essais ont été réalisés à partir de radiographies de la Polyclinique Pasteur de Bamako, avec notamment la détection de plus de 200 cas de cardiomégalie modérée. Cette innovation a valu à Maimouna Dicko la deuxième place du concours Youth Innov-Tech, organisé dans le cadre du Transform Africa Summit 2025, rendez-vous continental consacré à l’innovation numérique et placé sous le thème « AI for Africa : Innovate Locally, Impact Globally ». Cette distinction souligne le potentiel des jeunes innovateurs africains à développer des solutions adaptées aux réalités locales tout en répondant à des enjeux de santé publique. À travers MDS Health, Maimouna Dicko illustre la capacité de la recherche et des compétences maliennes à produire des innovations de pointe en Intelligence Artificielle. Son parcours témoigne également de l’importance d’investir dans les technologies de santé afin de renforcer l’accès à des diagnostics de qualité et d’accompagner la modernisation des systèmes de soins en Afrique. IBRAHIM BABY

items.title: FO says PM to visit Iran, Turkiye from July 3-5; to attend funeral of assassinated Iranian supreme leader
items.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif will visit Iran and Turkiye from July 3 to July 5, Foreign Office (FO) spokesperson Tahir Andrabi confirmed on Thursday. Addressing a weekly briefing, Andrabi said that during his visit to Iran, the premier would participate in the funeral of late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei , who was assassinated in US-Israeli strikes on February 28. The FO spokesperson said PM Shehbaz will travel to Iran first for Khamenei’s funeral. Deputy PM and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, cabinet members and senior officials will accompany the premier on his visit. “The prime minister will convey condolences on behalf of the people and government of Pakistan to the Iranian leadership and the bereaved families while reaffirming solidarity with the brotherly nation in their hour of profound grief,” he said. PM Shehbaz will then visit Istanbul at the invitation of Turkiye’s President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, Andrabi said. He added that the premier will “hold discussions on the entire gamut of bilateral relations with a special focus on giving impetus to bilateral trade and investment cooperation between the two brotherly countries”. “The leadership meeting will also reflect on issues concerning regional peace and security.” The prime minister will also address a business conference hosted by Pakistan to “spotlight Pakistan’s trade and investment potential in priority areas, including SEZs (special economic zones), energy, trade, IT and privatisation sectors”, Andrabi said. He noted that the conference would bring together leading Turkish businessmen and investors alongside senior officials, dignitaries and other distinguished participants from the business community. The FO spokesperson highlighted that PM Shehbaz’s visits to Iran and Turkiye “reflect Pakistan’s deep-rooted, historic, cultural and fraternal ties with the two brotherly nations”. Speaking about the US-Iran war, Andrabi noted that Islamabad has “stepped up its diplomatic engagement with key regional and international stakeholders as well as the principal interlocutors to facilitate earnest follow-up and implementation” of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding signed between the warring parties on June 18. Under the 14-point accord , Washington and Tehran agreed on a framework to end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and negotiate on key issues within 60 days of the signing. Andrabi highlighted that “positive progress [was] made on issues related to the different aspects of the Islamabad MoU” during the US-Iran talks held in Doha on Tuesday, which continued into the morning. The spokesperson affirmed that Pakistan will “continue to play a facilitative and mediatory role in the negotiation process along with our Qatari partners”. He recalled that in that regard, Dar also continued with “high-level consultations” as he spoke to several counterparts on the phone in recent days. Water is ‘not a tool of coercion’ During his briefing, the FO spokesperson also assailed India’s attempt to treat the Indus river system as a “strategic asset that can be controlled”. Andrabi declared that Pakistan rejected “India’s attempt to invoke baseless allegations of terrorism as a pretext for placing the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in abeyance and obstructing the lawful flow of the Pakistani shares” of the waters. “Let this be very clear: the real issue is not terrorism. The real issue is the growing disposition within the indian leadership to treat a shared international river system as a strategic asset that can be controlled, withheld or diverted at will,” he asserted. “This mindset of unilateral appropriation is fundamentally incompatible with the letter and spirit of not just the international law but also the IWT,” Andrabi said. Maintaining that water was “not a tool of coercion or political pressure”, he warned that any attempt to deny Pakistan its legitimate share of the Indus waters constituted a clear violation of the international legal obligations undertaken by India and “undermined India’s credibility of its commitment to a treaty-based relationship”. He noted that a summit , titled “International Seminar on Indus Waters Treaty: An Instrument of Peace and Regional Stability”, was held in Islamabad on Tuesday, where participants called for upholding the IWT and “rejected weaponisation of water”. Speakers, including Deputy PM Dar and international scholars, urged settling disputes through legal and diplomatic mechanisms rather than unilateral actions, Andrabi said. “They warned that any attempt to deprive the country of its shared water would have profound consequences for regional peace and security,” he added. The FO official mentioned Dar pointing out that the six-decade-old IWT could not be suspended or terminated under any pretext and that the abeyance was “illegal, unilateral and without any basis”. Andrabi also gave a firm response to a query about whether the summit could prevent Pakistan from being “converted into barren land”. “No country can do that. Not India, not any other country has the power to do that.” Andrabi was also asked about Islamabad’s response to a recent civil society letter written to the Pakistani and Indian premiers calling for steps to restore peace and dialogue between the neighbours. Former ambassador Ashraf Jehangir Qazi and senior politician Farhatullah Babar were among the signatories from Pakistan, while those on the Indian side included Dr Farooq Abdullah, Mirwaiz Umar Farooq and Mehbooba Mufti. The FO spokesperson said that while the “private individuals are fully competent to write anything”, the Pakistani government or FO “neither endorse nor reject” it and did not have any particular comment on the matter. On a discrepancy pointed out in the list of prisoners exchanged between Islamabad and New Delhi this week, Andrabi said the list handed over to India of 753 Pakistani prisoners in its custody was “meticulously compiled” and considered to be authentic. India, however, had shared a list of 439 prisoners who were “Pakistani or believed to be Pakistani”. Andrabi stressed that Pakistan’s list documented “all reported cases of Pakistani nationals detained in India, including those identified through newspaper reports, notifications from families and information received from official channels”. Unapproved action on Punjab gurdwara halted, building was not in use: FO Asked about the Indian foreign ministry’s comments on the reported demolition of a gurdwara in Punjab’s Farooqabad, the FO spokesperson said the place was not used for worship anymore and posed a risk as it was dilapidated. However, since the action was undertaken by tenants “without the permission of the Evacuee Trust Property Board (ETPB)”, the tenancy was cancelled and possible renovation work would be carried out, Andrabi said. “Immediate remedial action has started and was taken to address the situation. The ETPB halted the tenants’ unauthorised work on the same day on June 24,” he said, adding that while some part of the building was affected, action on the larger building was prevented. Noting that the tenants were directed to vacate the building, Andrabi said, “We would ensure that the building stays and some renovation work is also possible.” The FO spokesperson recalled that the tenants had initiated work on two sections of the seven-part gurdwara on June 24 as a “preventative measure” as the structure was in a dilapidated condition. “The structure was old and in a dilapidated condition and posed a potential risk to human lives and surrounding private properties […] they feared that the building might collapse in the upcoming rainy season,” he said, noting there was fear in the local community after the roof collapse at a Lahore tuition centre . Andrabi stated that the building was under private tenancy as there was “no Sikh community in the vicinity that would use the gurdwara for its religious purposes”.

items.title: Affaire du supporter algérien supposément agressé à Boston : un récit construit avant toute vérification
items.description: L'affaire du jeune supporter algérien qui aurait été agressé par des supporters marocains dans une fan zone à Boston, au lendemain du huitième de finale de la Coupe du monde entre le Maroc et les Pays-Bas, s'est imposée en quelques heures comme l'un des principaux sujets médiatiques en Algérie. Pourtant, plusieurs jours après les faits supposés, aucune autorité américaine n'a confirmé publiquement les éléments les plus graves avancés, tandis que de nombreuses incohérences entourent encore le récit largement diffusé. L'épisode illustre la rapidité avec laquelle une information non vérifiée peut être transformée en sujet politique majeur, au point d'être évoquée par le président algérien Abdelmadjid Tebboune avant même qu'une enquête indépendante ou une communication officielle des autorités américaines ne vienne corroborer les accusations. Une campagne médiatique portée par l'émotion avant la vérification La chronologie de cette affaire montre que le récit ne s'est pas construit à partir de sources policières ou judiciaires américaines, mais essentiellement par un effet de caisse de résonance entre les réseaux sociaux et plusieurs médias algériens. Les premières publications ont rapidement été relayées sans qu'aucune source officielle américaine ne confirme les faits rapportés. La diffusion du récit a ainsi largement précédé toute démarche de vérification indépendante, favorisant une propagation rapide d'allégations dont plusieurs éléments demeurent aujourd'hui non établis. Un récit qui évolue au fil de sa diffusion L'un des aspects les plus frappants de cette affaire réside dans les nombreuses variations observées au fur et à mesure de sa médiatisation. Le nombre de personnes présentées comme impliquées est notamment passé de six, puis sept, avant d'atteindre trente-cinq individus selon certains relais médiatiques. Les informations relatives aux arrestations, aux poursuites judiciaires ou encore au rôle du consulat algérien ont elles aussi évolué au gré des publications, sans qu'aucun document officiel américain ne vienne confirmer ces différentes versions. Ces évolutions successives interrogent sur la solidité des informations initialement diffusées. Aucune confirmation publique des autorités américaines À ce jour, la police de Boston n'a publié aucun communiqué confirmant le scénario d'une agression collective impliquant plusieurs dizaines de supporters lors d'une fan zone de la Coupe du monde. De la même manière, aucune autorité judiciaire américaine n'a communiqué sur une procédure correspondant au récit relayé dans plusieurs médias algériens. Autre élément notable, les principaux médias locaux américains n'ont pas non plus rapporté une affaire correspondant à cette version des faits, alors même qu'un incident d'une telle ampleur aurait vraisemblablement suscité une importante couverture médiatique locale. Des supports visuels qui soulèvent des interrogations Les contenus utilisés pour illustrer cette affaire présentent également plusieurs incohérences. Une photographie largement relayée comporte notamment des éléments visuels renvoyant à la Coupe du monde 2022 au Qatar, ce qui interroge sur son utilisation pour illustrer des faits supposément survenus à Boston en 2026. La vidéo devenue virale ne permet pas davantage d'établir avec certitude le récit avancé. Elle juxtapose plusieurs séquences distinctes sans continuité permettant d'affirmer qu'elles concernent la même personne ou les mêmes événements. Par ailleurs, si plusieurs analyses situent effectivement certaines images au niveau de The Tall Ship , à East Boston, cette géolocalisation ne permet en aucun cas de confirmer l'identité de la victime, celle des personnes impliquées ni leur nationalité. Identifier un lieu ne revient pas à authentifier le récit qui accompagne une vidéo. Une amplification politique particulièrement rapide Avant toute confirmation indépendante, l'affaire a rapidement été reprise par l'ensemble de l'écosystème médiatique algérien. Son évocation par le président Abdelmadjid Tebboune a ensuite conféré au dossier une dimension institutionnelle, renforçant encore sa visibilité et lui donnant le statut d'une véritable affaire d'État. Cette séquence illustre la vitesse avec laquelle un récit médiatique peut être élevé au rang de sujet politique sans que les éléments matériels aient été préalablement établis. Une campagne dont l'origine apparaît identifiable L'analyse de la chronologie des publications montre que la campagne est partie d'un nombre restreint de comptes particulièrement actifs avant d'être reprise successivement par plusieurs médias, puis par les autorités. Plusieurs de ces relais sont déjà connus pour diffuser régulièrement des contenus hostiles au Maroc. Parmi eux figure notamment Ahmed Hafsi, considéré par ses détracteurs comme l'un des principaux propagandistes proches de la présidence algérienne. Même des voix critiques envers Rabat contestent le récit Fait notable, les interrogations sur la crédibilité de cette affaire ne proviennent pas uniquement de médias favorables au Maroc. Le journaliste marocain Mohamed Elmoussaoui, connu pour ses positions très critiques à l'égard des autorités marocaines et pour sa proximité avec le Mouvement du 20 février, est lui aussi arrivé à la conclusion qu'aucune source américaine ouverte ne permettait, à ce stade, de confirmer la version largement diffusée en Algérie. Cette convergence de constats, émanant d'acteurs aux sensibilités très différentes, renforce les interrogations entourant le récit initial. Distinguer les faits établis des allégations À ce stade, rien ne permet d'exclure qu'une altercation ait pu se produire en marge des festivités liées à la Coupe du monde. En revanche, les affirmations selon lesquelles un mineur aurait été violemment agressé par plusieurs dizaines de supporters marocains, que des dizaines de suspects auraient été identifiés ou que d'importantes poursuites judiciaires seraient engagées ne sont, pour l'heure, corroborées par aucune source publique américaine. Cette distinction entre faits établis et allégations demeure essentielle dans l'analyse du dossier. Un schéma classique de guerre informationnelle Au-delà de l'incident lui-même, cette affaire illustre un mode opératoire devenu récurrent dans les campagnes informationnelles visant le Maroc. Le processus suit une mécanique bien identifiée : partir d'un événement aux contours incertains, lui ajouter progressivement des éléments émotionnels, faire évoluer les chiffres au fil des reprises, mobiliser massivement les réseaux sociaux et les hashtags, avant de donner au récit une portée politique, souvent avant que les vérifications indépendantes puissent être réalisées. L'objectif paraît ainsi moins de documenter précisément un incident que d'imposer rapidement une narration susceptible d'influencer l'opinion publique et de porter atteinte à l'image du Maroc. The post Affaire du supporter algérien supposément agressé à Boston : un récit construit avant toute vérification appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Mairie de Dabola : Mamadou Condé “Pascal” (RGP) élu maire face au candidat de la GMD
items.description: Ce jeudi 2 juillet, à l’issue du vote des conseillers communaux, Mamadou Condé, dit « Pascal », candidat du Rassemblement pour la Guinée Prospère (RGP), a été élu maire de la commune urbaine de Dabola. Le scrutin s’est soldé par une victoire nette du camp du RGP, formation de l’ancien président Alpha Condé. Sur les suffrages exprimés par les conseillers communaux : Mamadou Condé (RGP) : 20 voix Alpha Ibrahima Diallo (GMD) : 12 voix Le candidat de la Génération pour la Modernité et le Développement (GMD) n’a pas réussi à inverser la tendance, laissant ainsi la gestion de cette commune stratégique de la Haute-Guinée au RGP. Dans sa première prise de parole après son élection, le nouveau maire a tendu la main à l’ensemble des conseillers communaux. « Nous tendons la main à tous pour le développement de Dabola. Nous appelons déjà à une union sacrée pour le développement de la préfecture. Une nouvelle ère commence pour la municipalité de Dabola », a-t-il déclaré.

items.title: Carburant : La parole manque à la pompe
items.description: © JDM À Bamako, les files devant les stations-service sont devenues presque ordinaires. Des citernes arrivent, parfois par centaines, escortées par les forces armées, mais des usagers cherchent encore de l’essence ou du gasoil pendant des heures. Certaines stations restent fermées, d’autres sont prises d’assaut dès qu’un camion est annoncé. Entre l’arrivée des produits et leur disponibilité réelle, une zone d’ombre nourrit l’inquiétude. Il faut reconnaître que des efforts sont faits. Les convois sont sécurisés, les stocks suivis, les autorités communiquent sur les volumes acheminés et les difficultés logistiques. Mais encore faut-il que le terrain suive. Sinon, l’effort donne l’impression de transpirer sous la pluie : beaucoup d’énergie dépensée, peu de soulagement pour celui qui attend le liquide précieux avec son réservoir ou son bidon. Certes, la situation n’est pas celle de septembre 2025, lorsque des citernes incendiées avaient provoqué une crise plus grave. Mais le malaise demeure. Le carburant conditionne le transport, les marchés, les hôpitaux, les groupes électrogènes, les écoles, les prix et le travail quotidien. Quand il manque, tout ralentit. Quand l’information manque aussi, chacun cherche une explication ailleurs. Dans une période où les rumeurs circulent vite, le silence devient un risque. Informer régulièrement ne signifie pas tout justifier. Cela signifie dire où sont les blocages, quels volumes sont disponibles, quelles stations sont servies et quelles mesures évitent les abus. La confiance se construit à la pompe lorsque la parole publique rejoint la réalité vécue. Dans une crise durable, l’explication ne doit pas être un luxe. Elle est une partie de la réponse, au même titre que l’approvisionnement attendu par les citoyens.

items.title: Ghana National Council of Metropolitan Chicago launches GhanaFest® 2026 with historic first-ever Ghana flag-raising ceremony
items.description: The Ghana National Council of Metropolitan Chicago (GNC) has officially launched the 2026 GhanaFest® season with the city’s first-ever ceremonial raising of the Ghanaian flag in downtown Chicago, marking Ghana’s Republic Day and setting the stage for the 37th edition of one of the largest Ghanaian cultural festivals in the United States. The landmark flag-raising ceremony, held on July 1 at Daley Plaza, brought together elected officials, community leaders, dignitaries and residents to commemorate Ghana’s transition to a sovereign republic while officially launching the countdown to this year’s GhanaFest®. According to the organisers, the event marked the first time the Ghanaian flag had been ceremonially raised in downtown Chicago, highlighting the growing influence and visibility of the Ghanaian community in the city. The 37th Annual GhanaFest® will take place on Saturday, July 25, from 10:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. at Washington Park, located at 5600 South Russell Drive in Chicago, Illinois. This year’s festival will be held under the theme, “Connecting Our Community Through Culture and Commerce,” reflecting the organisers’ commitment to promoting cultural appreciation, economic empowerment and community engagement. The event is being organised by the Ghana National Council of Metropolitan Chicago, its affiliate organisations and PassportPay. About GhanaFest Returning after a one-year hiatus, GhanaFest® is expected to attract more than 6,000 visitors for a day-long celebration of Ghanaian culture, featuring authentic cuisine, music, dance, fashion, arts and cultural exhibitions. For nearly four decades, the festival has served as a platform to celebrate Ghana’s rich cultural heritage while strengthening ties within the Ghanaian community and the wider African diaspora. Originally established almost 38 years ago as a small community gathering in Jackson Park, GhanaFest® has grown into one of the largest Ghanaian cultural celebrations in the United States and has inspired similar festivals in cities with sizeable Ghanaian populations across the country and internationally. This year’s festival will feature authentic Ghanaian cuisine prepared by some of Chicagoland’s leading Ghanaian restaurants and food vendors, alongside exhibitions of traditional clothing, jewellery, artwork, beauty products and handcrafted items. Visitors will also have the opportunity to explore cultural and educational exhibitions highlighting Ghana’s history and traditions, as well as a marketplace showcasing artisans, local businesses and community organisations. Family-friendly activities have also been planned at the Youth Village, including dance competitions, Ghana-themed trivia, games, bounce houses and other interactive experiences. The festival programme will include live performances by internationally acclaimed musicians and entertainers, as well as the much-anticipated Parade of Chiefs and Queen Mothers representing all 16 regions of Ghana. The celebration will conclude with an evening concert featuring performances for audiences of all ages. The organisers say this year’s festival builds on efforts to strengthen partnerships and broaden engagement beyond the Ghanaian community. Under the leadership of GNC President Effah Ameyaw, who is serving his first year in office, the Council has prioritised expanding collaboration with businesses, organisations and communities to promote cultural exchange and increase awareness of Ghanaian heritage. PassportPay, one of the event’s principal sponsors, says supporting GhanaFest® aligns with its commitment to community development and economic empowerment. “At PassportPay, we believe that investing in communities is just as important as investing in innovation,” said Kofi Conduah, Founder of PassportPay. “GhanaFest® is a remarkable celebration of culture, entrepreneurship, and community that brings together people from all walks of life. We are proud to sponsor an event that not only honours the rich heritage of the Ghanaian community but also creates opportunities for small businesses, entrepreneurs, and families to connect and thrive. Supporting GhanaFest® aligns with our mission of empowering communities through meaningful partnerships and helping build stronger economic opportunities for generations to come.” Tickets for the festival are now available online here . About the Ghana National Council of Metropolitan Chicago The Ghana National Council of Metropolitan Chicago is a community-based organisation dedicated to promoting unity, civic engagement, cultural preservation and the advancement of Ghanaians across the Greater Chicago area. Through advocacy, cultural programming and strategic partnerships, the Council continues to strengthen the Ghanaian community while promoting Ghana’s heritage and contributions in the United States.

items.title: Lambanyi : Baba Alimou Barry élu maire de la commune urbaine
items.description: La commune urbaine de Lambanyi a officiellement un nouveau maire. À l’issue de l’élection organisée ce jeudi, dans le cadre de l’installation des exécutifs communaux à travers le pays, Baba Alimou Barry, tête de liste de la coalition GMD (Bâtir Ensemble), a été porté à la tête de la mairie. Il s’est imposé face à son principal challenger, Morlaye Camara, candidat de l’UFC, en obtenant la majorité absolue des suffrages exprimés par les conseillers communaux. Président du Mouvement pour l’Alternance en Guinée (MAG), Baba Alimou Barry prend ainsi les commandes de l’une des plus grandes communes de Conakry. Son élection marque une nouvelle étape pour Lambanyi, où les attentes des populations restent élevées. Le nouveau maire et son équipe sont désormais attendus sur la mise en œuvre des engagements pris devant les électeurs.

Amaechi meets Tambuwal in Abuja amid 2027 ADC consultations
items.description: ABUJA — The vice-presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) for the 2027 general election, Rotimi Amaechi, on Thursday held a closed-door meeting with former Sokoto State Governor, Aminu Tambuwal, in Abuja. Although details of the meeting were not immediately disclosed, it comes amid ongoing political consultations ahead of the 2027 general elections. The meeting also followed the upload of the names of the ADC’s presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, and his running mate, Amaechi, on the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) portal as part of preparations for the 2027 elections. Amaechi was recently unveiled by the ADC as the party’s vice-presidential candidate, completing the party’s presidential ticket for the forthcoming polls. Thursday’s meeting also came shortly after a Federal High Court in Abuja affirmed the leadership of former Senate President David Mark as National Chairman of the ADC. While the outcome of the Amaechi-Tambuwal meeting was not made public as of press time, political observers believe it is part of ongoing consultations and strategic engagements within the party ahead of the 2027 elections. The post Amaechi meets Tambuwal in Abuja amid 2027 ADC consultations appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Public health officers push for face masks and handwashing amid post-flood risks
items.description: The Ghana Association of Public Health Technical Officers (GAPHTO) has called on the government to immediately reinforce public health safety measures, including compulsory handwashing and the use of face masks, to reduce the risk of disease outbreaks in communities affected by the recent floods. The National President of the association, Joseph Owusu Asante, said although emergency response efforts were rightly focused on rescue operations and relief for flood victims, equal attention must now be given to preventing secondary public health emergencies. Speaking on JoyNews’ The Pulse , Mr Owusu Asante stressed that floodwaters often carry a cocktail of disease-causing organisms capable of triggering outbreaks of cholera, typhoid fever, diarrhoeal diseases, leptospirosis and other waterborne infections if adequate preventive measures were not enforced. “I think we would want to entreat government that in doing all this we also have to ensure that there’s a bit of policy behind what is going on.” He proposed that some of the public health measures adopted during the COVID-19 pandemic be temporarily revived in flood-hit communities. “One of them is the hand washing and the others like the use of nose masks must be introduced and made as one of the major community barriers to make sure that pathogens that are mixed up do not get a chance to thrive and cause outbreaks within these times.” Danger of contaminated floodwater Mr Owusu Asante also cautioned residents against using floodwater to wash household items or clean their homes, describing the practice as highly dangerous because the water is heavily contaminated. His comments follow reports and video footage showing some residents in flood-affected communities using stagnant floodwater during cleanup exercises after Monday’s torrential rains inundated several parts of Accra and other communities. He explained that floodwater contains sewage, industrial waste, chemicals and numerous microorganisms capable of causing serious illness. “Not at all. Not at all. And I think providing water is one of the basics.” He emphasised that even hands that appear clean can harbour harmful microorganisms, making visibly polluted floodwater an even greater health hazard. “In fact, even your hands that do not appear dirty carry pathogens or disease-causing organisms. How much more floodwater that is obviously looking turbid and seriously contaminated, and would carry so much to harm your life.” Protective equipment The public health expert urged authorities to provide adequate personal protective equipment (PPE) for sanitation workers and volunteers engaged in post-flood cleanup operations. He said where residents had no option but to participate in cleaning exercises, they should wear protective clothing and observe strict hygiene practices to minimise exposure to contaminants. “We must get people PPEs to be doing this work. Otherwise, if you have to do that at home, then you must observe a high level of hygiene practice.” Mr Owusu Asante warned that relying on contaminated floodwater for cleaning could expose survivors to serious health complications even after escaping the immediate effects of the disaster. “You want to use the contaminated water for your cleaning. You just trying to make the worst happen to you after you have survived the disaster. That is not advisable.” Heightened health surveillance The appeal comes as health authorities intensify surveillance following Monday’s devastating floods, which claimed lives, displaced hundreds of residents and submerged homes, businesses and major roads across the Greater Accra Region. Public health experts have repeatedly warned that the days immediately after flooding present the greatest risk for disease outbreaks due to contaminated water sources, damaged sanitation facilities and increased exposure to infectious pathogens. They have therefore urged the public to consume only safe drinking water, maintain proper hand hygiene, avoid contact with contaminated floodwater where possible and promptly report symptoms of diarrhoea, vomiting or fever to the nearest health facility.

items.title: Lahore police register case against five suspects over abduction, sexual assault of two foreign women
items.description: LAHORE: Police in the provincial capital on Thursday registered a case against five suspects on charges of kidnapping for ransom and sexually assaulting two foreign women. The Police Emergency Helpline 15 received a call from Spain, made by the father of one of the women, reporting the incident. The alleged victims, who are nationals of the Netherlands and Venezuela, had come to Pakistan to visit their ‘friends’. DIG Faisal Kamran told Dawn that police acted accordingly, dispatched a team of senior officials, recovered the women, arrested four suspects and lodged an FIR. He said the suspects had been tracked and apprehended with the help of the Punjab Safe City Authority’s cameras installed in the city. The recovered foreign women were later produced before a judicial magistrate at Cantonment Courts to record their statements under Section 164 of the Code of Criminal Procedure. A women’s counsel for the victims was also present in the courtroom during the recording of their statements. The victims narrated the incident before the magistrate in a closed-door hearing. “We took into confidence the Dutch Embassy officials and updated them about the criminal proceedings of the case, recovery of the victims”, the DIG said, adding that the travel documents were completed and the women would fly at the earliest from Pakistan. He said a security guard had escaped from the scene, and police teams were trying to trace his location. DIG Kamran said investigations were underway, adding that those who committed crimes against the women must be brought before a court of law and punished under the law of the land. According to the FIR, the women were abducted by five suspects, including a close relative of a senior political personality, who demanded ransom and subjected them to sexual assault during their captivity. The suspects allegedly demanded $1.5 million in ransom before sexually assaulting them. The Punjab government said Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz took immediate notice of the abduction of foreign women, following which Lahore police launched a swift operation and rescued the victims within two hours, arresting four suspects. According to a handout, the chief minister directed the police to recover the women within two hours of being informed about the incident. It said police immediately launched a rescue operation after receiving a report from the victim’s father. It said the suspects were traced with the assistance of the Safe City Authority, leading to the recovery of the abducted women. The rescued women underwent medical examinations, and further legal proceedings are under way in light of the available evidence, it added.

items.title: Sécurité routière : l’Afrique cherche une réponse coordonnée
items.description: © Internet L’Afrique reste la région du monde la plus exposée aux décès routiers. Les dernières données continentales estiment à plus de 259 000 le nombre de morts sur les routes en 2021, soit près du quart du bilan mondial, alors que le continent possède une part limitée du parc automobile. Cette mortalité frappe d’abord les usagers les plus vulnérables. Piétons, motocyclistes et cyclistes représentent plus de la moitié des décès recensés. Les piétons comptent à eux seuls près d’un tiers des victimes, dans des villes où trottoirs, passages protégés, éclairage et transports collectifs restent souvent insuffisants. Les causes se répètent d’un pays à l’autre. Vitesse, surcharge, fatigue des chauffeurs, non-respect du code, faiblesse des contrôles, importation de véhicules vétustes, routes dégradées et absence d’aires de repos rendent les déplacements dangereux, surtout sur les grands corridors régionaux. En Afrique de l’Ouest, ces facteurs pèsent sur les axes interétatiques comme Bamako-Dakar, où transport de passagers, camions, deux-roues et véhicules particuliers partagent des routes très sollicitées. Au Sénégal, l’Agence nationale de sécurité routière évoque en moyenne 5 200 accidents par an, 745 décès et 8 500 blessés graves, pour un coût économique estimé à 163 milliards de francs CFA. Réponses communes La réponse ne peut plus rester seulement nationale. Les États cherchent à harmoniser les règles, les sanctions, les contrôles et la collecte de données. La CEDEAO travaille sur la gouvernance des transports, tandis que l’Union africaine a soutenu la création, en 2018, de l’Observatoire africain de la sécurité routière. Cet observatoire vise à améliorer la qualité des statistiques, longtemps fragilisées par les écarts entre bilans officiels, données policières, données hospitalières et estimations internationales. Selon le SSATP, moins de la moitié des pays africains disposent de bases nationales opérationnelles sur les accidents, et seuls 23 utilisent la définition internationale de la mortalité à 30 jours. Des réponses nationales se structurent aussi. Le Burkina Faso a validé une stratégie 2026-2030, avec l’objectif de réduire d’au moins 50 % les décès routiers d’ici 2030, après une moyenne annuelle de 23 107 accidents, 14 867 blessés et 1 091 morts entre 2020 et 2024. La sécurité routière devient ainsi un chantier africain commun. Elle exige des données fiables, des routes mieux conçues, des véhicules contrôlés, des conducteurs formés et des sanctions appliquées avec constance.

items.title: US Embassy in Abuja, Lagos Consulate shut Friday for Independence Day
items.description: The United States Embassy in Abuja and the Consulate General in Lagos will be closed on Friday, July 3, in observance of the United States Independence Day. The US Mission in Nigeria announced the temporary closure in a post shared on its official social media platforms on Thursday. “The US Embassy in Abuja and Consulate General in Lagos will be closed on Friday, July 3, 2026, in observance of US Independence Day,” the mission said. The closure is in line with the annual celebration of the United States’ Independence Day, popularly known as the Fourth of July, which is marked every year on July 4. The day commemorates the adoption of the United States Declaration of Independence in 1776, when the thirteen American colonies formally declared their independence from Great Britain, laying the foundation for the establishment of the United States as a sovereign nation. The post US Embassy in Abuja, Lagos Consulate shut Friday for Independence Day appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: USTED, KNUST Host SFA Foundation team for NEPS Youth Mental Health Project review
items.description: Researchers from the University of Skills Training and Entrepreneurial Development (USTED), in collaboration with the Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology, Kumasi (KNUST), have hosted a delegation from the Science for Africa Foundation (SFA Foundation) as part of the Ghana site visit and implementation review for the Navigating Educational Pressures and Stressors (NEPS) Project. The engagement brought together the NEPS Ghana research team and representatives of the SFA Foundation to review project governance, implementation progress, institutional roles, partner engagement, financial management, reporting indicators, emerging challenges and next steps. USTED serves as the host institution for the NEPS Project, while KNUST is a key consortium partner, providing scientific, technical and implementation leadership across selected work packages. NEPS is a 42-month, multi-country youth mental health research project being implemented in Ghana, Sierra Leone and Tanzania. The project examines how educational pressure, poverty, stigma and related psychosocial stressors affect the mental wellbeing of adolescents and young people while generating evidence to inform school, community and policy-level interventions. The project brings together research teams from the three countries to produce context-specific evidence on youth mental health, educational stressors and practical support systems aimed at strengthening adolescent wellbeing. The visit formed part of the SFA Foundation’s engagement with NEPS under its BEING Initiative, an international mental health programme focused on improving the wellbeing of young people aged 10 to 24. The SFA Foundation delegation travelled from Kenya and was led by Programme Manager Mr. Byron Bitanihirwe. Other members included Dr. Leah Aoko, Programme Officer; Mr. Benard Kituku, Specialist, Programme Finance; Ms. Judy Mwaniki, Programme Officer for Public Engagement with Science (PES); Mr. Brian Awiti, Specialist, Programme Finance; and Mr. Kelvin Kasoli, Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability and Learning (MEAL) Officer. Vice-Chancellor of USTED, the Pro Vice-Chancellor, Prof. Isaac Boateng. Addressing participants on behalf of the Vice-Chancellor of USTED, the Pro Vice-Chancellor, Prof. Isaac Boateng, underscored the importance of the project in addressing the growing mental and emotional pressures confronting students in educational institutions. “The Navigating Educational Pressures and Stressors Project seeks to deepen our understanding of the realities facing students and learners within our educational systems,” he said. Prof. Boateng said the project was expected to generate practical, evidence-based solutions to inform policy, institutional practice and support systems that promote student wellbeing and academic success. “More importantly, it aims to generate evidence-based solutions that can inform policies, institutional practice, and support mechanisms that promote students’ well-being and academic success,” he said. He said the significance of the project extended beyond individual institutions and aligned with Ghana’s national development agenda and the aspirations of the African Union’s Agenda 2063. “The significance of this work extends far beyond individual institutions. It speaks directly to Ghana’s National Development Agenda and Africa’s broader aspiration under Agenda 2063, which envisions a prosperous Africa driven by its people, particularly its youth,” he added. Prof. Boateng noted that students today face increasing pressures from academic demands, social expectations, financial challenges, technological change, family responsibilities and uncertainty about future employment. He cautioned that, if left unaddressed, these stressors could adversely affect learning outcomes, student retention, innovation and long-term development. Members of the Ghana project team who participated in the review included Prof. Obed Brew, Prof. Daniel Ansong, Prof. Linda Ahenkorah Fondjo, Mr. Robert Anohene, Miss Akosua Gyamfua, Dr. Linda Banning, Dr. Kotor Asare, and Dr. Jeff Boakye.

items.title: Mondial 2026: L’Anglais Michael Oliver désigné pour diriger le 8è Canada-Maroc
items.description: L'arbitre anglais Michael Oliver a été désigné pour officier la rencontre opposant le Canada au Maroc, prévue samedi au Houston Stadium, pour le compte des huitièmes de finale de la Coupe du Monde 2026. items.title: Tinubu: Economic reforms have ended states’ reliance on salary borrowing
items.description: …FG flags off Babban-Lamba–Sharam Phase II Road in Plateau By Golok Nanmwa JOS — President Bola Tinubu has said his administration’s economic reforms have freed state governments from borrowing to pay workers’ salaries, attributing the development to increased revenue allocations that now enable states to meet salary obligations, settle gratuities and undertake development projects. The President, represented by the National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Prof. Nentawe Yilwatda, made the remarks on Thursday during the flag-off of the rehabilitation of the Babban-Lamba–Sharam Phase II Road in Kanke Local Government Area of Plateau State. Tinubu said the administration’s reforms had strengthened the economy and significantly improved revenues accruing to states. “Our economic reforms are improving governance because all the economic indicators are pointing in a positive direction, from our GDP to our foreign reserves and other economic indices. “Nigeria has turned from amber to green. These are positive signs for our economy. Today, more revenues are coming to the states. No state receives less than three to four times what it used to receive. “State revenues have increased, and states can now afford to execute projects without collecting loans or issuing bonds. “Before we came into office, over 30 states were owing salaries. Today, no state is owing salaries because the improved revenue allows them to pay salaries, gratuities and also embark on developmental projects,” he said. Tinubu described the Babban-Lamba–Sharam Phase II Road as a strategic federal highway that would stimulate economic growth by linking Plateau with Nasarawa, Kaduna, Gombe and Adamawa states, while strengthening trade and movement across the North-Central and North-East. He said the project forms part of the administration’s legacy infrastructure programme aimed at connecting communities, improving security, reducing travel time and promoting inclusive economic development. “This project is not just a road. It will connect communities, businesses, families and farmlands. It will improve security, reduce travel time by over 100 kilometres and create prosperity across Northern Nigeria,” he said. The President disclosed that the Federal Government is currently executing 61 road projects across the North-Central geopolitical zone, with five completed, 25 major projects under construction and 31 emergency intervention projects ongoing. He also highlighted other flagship infrastructure projects of the administration, including the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway, the Badagry-Sokoto Super Highway and the Calabar-Abuja corridor, saying they would improve connectivity and economic integration across the country. Speaking at the ceremony, the Minister of Works, Senator David Umahi, said the Federal Government terminated the earlier contract covering the first 6.15 kilometres of the road due to the contractor’s poor performance. Umahi said the remaining 40 kilometres would be reconstructed using continuous reinforced concrete pavement, which he described as President Tinubu’s legacy road standard. “I was not satisfied with the quality of work and the delay. In line with the provisions of the contract, we terminated that section and commenced a new phase that will be delivered using reinforced concrete pavement for the benefit of future generations,” he said. He disclosed that the road would be fitted with solar-powered streetlights, security cameras, observation stations and tree planting to improve safety and environmental sustainability. The minister also assured the Plateau State Government that the Federal Government would consider constructing a bypass on the Akwanga-Jos Road to ease traffic congestion. Plateau State Governor Caleb Mutfwang commended President Tinubu for reviving the Babban-Lamba–Sharam Road project, which was first awarded in April 2018 but had remained largely dormant. He described the project as a major boost to economic activities in Plateau and neighbouring states. “No President has remembered Plateau in terms of infrastructure like President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. This road is more than a road; it is an opening of opportunities and a gateway to greater economic possibilities,” the governor said. Mutfwang added that the project, alongside the ongoing reconstruction of the Akwanga-Jos Road, would improve transportation, commerce and regional integration. He appealed to the Federal Government to prioritise the rehabilitation of the Jos–Mangu–Kanke–Shendam–Ibi Road linking Plateau and Taraba states, describing it as another strategic federal highway with significant economic potential. Also speaking, the member representing Pankshin/Kanke/Kanam Federal Constituency, Hon. Yusuf Gagdi, commended President Tinubu for prioritising infrastructure development and implementing policies aimed at repositioning the economy. He said the rehabilitation of the Babban-Lamba–Sharam Phase II Road demonstrated the administration’s commitment to equitable distribution of federal projects and would boost agriculture, commerce and other socio-economic activities in Plateau and neighbouring states. The Babban-Lamba–Sharam Phase II Road, first awarded on April 26, 2018, is expected to improve connectivity, facilitate the movement of goods and agricultural produce, and enhance socio-economic development across the North-Central and North-East regions when completed. The post Tinubu: Economic reforms have ended states’ reliance on salary borrowing appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.description: Le 31 mai dernier, les Guinéens ont élu les conseillers communaux du pays. Un mois après, ces conseillers élus des communes urbaines et rurales avaient rendez-vous avec les urnes pour procéder ce jeudi 2 juillet à l'élection leurs bureaux exécutifs respectifs. A pita, cette dernière étape électorale a donné les résultats suivants : Maire de la commune urbaine de Pita : Elhadj Safaiou Bah, issu de la liste de la GMD. Premier vice-maire : Amadou Djogo Bah, issu de la liste de la GMD. Deuxième vice-maire : Aïssatou Missidè Bah, issue de la liste de la GMD. Troisième vice-maire : Mamadou Yéro Barry, issu de la liste de la GMD. Quatrième vice-maire : Mamadou Atighou Doumbouya, issu de la liste de la GMD. En définitive, il convient de noter que l’ensemble des membres du bureau exécutif de la commune de Pita est issu de la GMD. Par ailleurs, aucune contestation n’a été enregistrée pour le moment. Ces élections marquent également la fin des fonctions des délégations spéciales.

items.title: Tunji-Ojo meets Assembly Speakers, seeks stronger Federal-State ties
items.description: By Omeiza Ajayi ABUJA: Minister of Interior, Dr Olubunmi Tunji-Ojo, has reaffirmed his commitment to fostering stronger institutional relationships with sub-national legislatures as key stakeholders in governance. The minister made the pledge on Thursday when he received the Conference of Speakers of State Legislatures of Nigeria, led by its Chairman and Speaker of the Delta State House of Assembly, Rt Hon Emomotimi Dennis Guwor, on a courtesy visit to the Ministry’s headquarters in Abuja. Head, Press and Public Relations in the Ministry, Mrs Mary Ali, said the minister commended the Conference for its proactive approach to partnership and nation-building, noting that sustainable reforms in internal security and civic administration require strong synergy between federal and state institutions. He assured the delegation that its requests and proposed areas of collaboration would receive due consideration within the framework of existing policies and national priorities, and reiterated the Ministry’s resolve to continue implementing people-centred reforms aimed at enhancing national security, improving border management, and strengthening the integrity of Nigeria’s internal governance systems. Speaking on behalf of the delegation, Guwor congratulated the Minister on his leadership and reform-driven initiatives at the Ministry, noting that his performance reflects the confidence reposed in him by President Bola Ahmed Tinubu. He emphasised that the Conference of Speakers, as representatives of sub-national legislatures closest to the grassroots, remains a critical partner in supporting policies and reforms that directly affect citizens. The Chairman noted the importance of strengthening the existing relationship between the Conference and the Ministry, particularly in areas that promote national cohesion, strengthen the country’s security architecture, and enhance efficient public service delivery. He affirmed the readiness of State Houses of Assembly to provide legislative support in advancing the Federal Government’s Renewed Hope Agenda. The post Tunji-Ojo meets Assembly Speakers, seeks stronger Federal-State ties appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: School abductions are national disgrace, crime against humanity — NSCIA
items.description: By Luminous Jannamike ABUJA — The Nigerian Supreme Council for Islamic Affairs (NSCIA) has described the recurring abduction and killing of schoolchildren in Nigeria as a ‘national disgrace’ and a ‘crime against humanity’, following recent attack on Government Day Secondary School in Lassa, Askira Uba Local Government Area of Borno state, where terrorists killed a teacher and abducted scores of students and staff. The attack has renewed fears over the safety of schools in insurgency-hit communities, where children and teachers remain vulnerable to abduction despite years of military operations. In a statement by its Public Affairs Officer, Abbas Jimoh, the council, led by its President-General, Muhammad Sa’ad Abubakar III, condemned the attack in the strongest terms, saying terrorism remains one of Nigeria’s greatest security threats and requires a firm, coordinated national response. “The Council is of the strong opinion that the unconscionable acts of kidnapping and murder of schoolchildren constitute a despicable crime against humanity. That these unfortunate incidents have continued to reoccur is a national disgrace that strikes at the very heart of Nigeria’s future and must be stopped immediately,” Jimoh said. The council said the latest attack followed the May 15 abduction of 42 pupils from Mussa Primary and Junior Secondary School in the same local government area. It also recalled the kidnapping of 49 pupils and their teachers in Oriire Local Government Area on the same day, noting that many of those victims are still being held. It said the attackers appeared to have exploited Lassa’s market day to slip into the community before launching the assault, a tactic that underscored how terrorist groups continue to adapt while exploiting security gaps. According to the NSCIA, “This demonstrates that terrorists continue to adapt their methods and exploit security gaps. Therefore, intelligence gathering, surveillance, inter-agency cooperation, and community-based early warning mechanisms must be significantly strengthened to reduce the vulnerability of our compatriots to terrorists’ attacks.” The council also urged governments to embrace technology-driven security measures, pointing to the recent upgrade of the security architecture by the Enugu State Government. It highlighted measures including an AI-powered Command and Control Centre, sophisticated surveillance drones and AI-enabled patrol vehicles designed to identify threats and track criminals in real time. Meanwhile, the council praised troops of Operation HADIN KAI for their swift deployment of ground and air assets, which led to the rescue of some of the abducted students and teachers. It, however, insisted that the operation should continue until every remaining victim is safely reunited with their families. Commending the security forces, the council said the operation “must not cease until every abducted child and member of staff are rescued alive and reunited with their families.” It also paid tribute to a soldier and a member of the Civilian Joint Task Force who lost their lives during the rescue mission, describing their sacrifice as a powerful demonstration of courage and patriotism. Reaffirming its position on violent extremism, the NSCIA said terrorist groups operating under the banners of Boko Haram and Islamic State West Africa Province have no connection with Islam, arguing that their actions run contrary to the religion’s teachings on the sanctity of life, justice, mercy and the pursuit of knowledge. “Boko Haram and ISWAP are enemies of Islam and humanity whose actions constitute a complete betrayal of the Qur’anic injunctions on the sanctity of human life, justice, mercy, and the pursuit of knowledge. Those who murder innocent people, abduct children, terrorise communities, and destroy schools cannot in any way claim to represent Islam or any recognised religious tradition. “Nigeria must never surrender to terror. Those who wage war against innocent citizens must be relentlessly pursued, brought to justice, and made to face the full weight of the law to ensure lasting peace and security throughout the country,” the apex Muslim body added. The council also conveyed its condolences to the government and people of Borno State, the management of Government Day Secondary School, Lassa, the families of the abducted students, and everyone affected by the latest attack. The post School abductions are national disgrace, crime against humanity — NSCIA appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: WHO declares Hantavirus outbreak over, warns of escalating Ebola crisis
items.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has officially declared the recent Hantavirus outbreak over. The declaration was made after the final person exposed aboard the cruise ship MV Hondius completed quarantine, tested negative, and returned home. Speaking during an online briefing on Thursday, WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Ghebreyesus said that no new infections have been recorded since May 25, marking the end of the international public health response to the outbreak. According to him, the outbreak resulted in 13 confirmed cases and 3 deaths. “Health authorities successfully identified and monitored more than 650 contacts across 33 countries and territories, helping to prevent further transmission through coordinated international surveillance and quarantine measures,” he said The body, however, noted that though it had declared the outbreak over, it emphasised that scientific investigations would continue. “The organisation is working with governments and research partners to improve understanding of Hantavirus transmission and disease progression in preparation for future outbreaks. “WHO is also coordinating a multinational study involving 21 countries to examine how Hantavirus develops in infected patients. “Findings from the study are expected to support the development of improved diagnostic tests, treatments, and vaccines,” he said. While celebrating the end of one outbreak, the director-general warned that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) was worsening. “The country has recorded an average of 38 new confirmed Ebola cases each day over the past two weeks, reflecting sustained community transmission. “As of July 2, DRC had confirmed 1,406 Ebola cases, including 438 deaths. “The outbreak remains one of the most challenging public health emergencies currently facing the region,” he said. According to him, the response has nevertheless expanded significantly. “Testing capacity has increased to 10 laboratories located closer to affected communities, allowing faster diagnosis and more efficient public health interventions. “Contact tracing has also improved, with approximately four out of every five identified contacts now being monitored,” he said. Ghebreyesus noted that more contacts still needed to be identified for each confirmed case to effectively interrupt transmission chains. He said that treatment capacity has grown to about 650 beds across 22 health centres, although roughly 96 percent of available beds are already occupied. “WHO and its partners are working to establish an additional 300 treatment beds to meet rising demand,” he said. He said another major milestone was the enrolment of the first patient in the PARTNERS clinical trial, which assesses the effectiveness of the monoclonal antibody MBP134 and the antiviral drug Remdesivir, administered individually and in combination. He said the study was coordinated by the DRC’s National Institute for Biomedical Research, in collaboration with WHO and international partners. “Patients participating in the clinical trial will receive comprehensive supportive medical care and close monitoring. “WHO also pledged to ensure broader access to the medicines should they prove safe and effective during the study. “WHO further announced the emergency use listing of the first molecular diagnostic test specifically designed to detect Bundibugyo virus, representing another step forward in strengthening outbreak preparedness and laboratory capacity,” he said. He, however, said that, despite these advances, the Ebola response continued to face serious obstacles, particularly insecurity and mistrust. According to him, an Ebola treatment centre in Ituri Province was recently attacked, leaving two people dead, forcing patients to flee, and destroying parts of the facility. He condemned the violence, stressing that attacks on health facilities endanger patients and frontline workers while undermining efforts to control disease transmission and save lives. “To strengthen coordination, WHO and the United Nations have appointed Julien Harneis as Senior Ebola Coordinator following a joint endorsement by WHO Director-General and UN Emergency Relief Coordinator, Tom Fletcher. Ghebreyesus also reported encouraging developments in Uganda, where no new Ebola cases have been recorded since June 21. “However, authorities have confirmed a case of Marburg virus disease in Kyegegwa District, detected through enhanced Ebola surveillance. “Health officials are monitoring all identified contacts, none of whom have developed symptoms so far,” he said. He said the concurrent outbreaks of Hantavirus, Ebola and Marburg highlighted the need for stronger international collaboration in tackling global health emergencies. He noted that WHO member states would resume negotiations next week on the Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing system under the WHO Pandemic Agreement, expressing confidence that countries remain committed to reaching consensus. “WHO expressed concern over the devastating earthquake in Venezuela, where more than 2,300 people have died, more than 5,000 have been injured, and nearly 16,000 have been left homeless. “WHO has released 1.5 million dollars from its Contingency Fund for Emergencies and dispatched more than six metric tons of emergency medical supplies, with an additional 28 metric tons expected shortly. “The organisation also highlighted air pollution as a persistent global health crisis that receives far less attention than disease outbreaks or natural disasters,” he said. According to him, the new WHO data indicate that 6.5 billion people remain exposed to dangerous levels of fine particulate matter (PM2.5), contributing to an estimated 6.7 million premature deaths annually. He said that the greatest burden falls on low- and middle-income countries, particularly in sub-Saharan and Northern Africa. Also, he drew attention to the growing health risks posed by digital environments to children and young people. He called for stronger regulation of social media platforms, warning that addictive platform designs, harmful online content, misinformation, and extensive data collection threaten children’s mental health, privacy, and overall well-being. The post WHO declares Hantavirus outbreak over, warns of escalating Ebola crisis appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Lambanyi : Baba Alimou prend les rênes de la commune et promet « mes ambitions, c’est de construire, de faire de Lambanyi la première référence en Guinée »
items.description: À la suite des élections communales et communautaires du 31 mai dernier en République de Guinée, les différentes mairies du pays ont procédé, ce jeudi 2 juillet, conformément à l’arrêté du ministère de l’Administration du Territoire et de la Décentralisation (MATD), à l’élection des maires et des vice-maires appelés à diriger les communes au cours des cinq prochaines années. À l’issue de ce scrutin, qui focalise aujourd’hui toutes les attentions en raison des tensions et des arrestations enregistrées dans certaines communes, la nouvelle commune de Lambanyi a élu son premier maire ainsi que ses sept vice-maires. Le processus s’est déroulé dans le calme, sans incident ni irrégularité, en présence des 45 conseillers communaux élus lors du scrutin du 31 mai dernier. À l’issue du dépouillement, le conseiller de la Génération pour la Modernité et le Développement (GMD), Baba Alimou Barry, est arrivé en tête et a, par conséquent, été élu maire de la commune de Lambanyi. Très enthousiaste à sa sortie du siège de la commune, le nouveau maire a salué le bon déroulement du scrutin : « Nous sortons des élections avec un cœur apaisé, dans l’unité, dans la joie, dans la paix à Lambanyi. Je félicite aujourd’hui tous les élus, les 45 conseillers au niveau de Lambanyi qui ont montré l’esprit d’entente, d’unité et de paix à Lambanyi. » Selon lui, « c’est une victoire. Je suis très heureux d’être là aujourd’hui et de vivre ce moment. Je suis très ému, je traverse une vive émotion, mais je ne peux pas dire grand-chose, sauf remercier la population de Lambanyi, remercier les autorités et remercier tous les acteurs qui ont concouru à faire de ce processus un processus apaisé d’union et de cohésion pour le développement ». Évoquant ses ambitions pour la commune, Baba Alimou Barry a promis : « De bâtir ensemble Lambanyi, main dans la main, dans l’intérêt supérieur de tous. Mes ambitions, c’est de construire Lambanyi, c’est de faire de Lambanyi la première référence en Guinée. C’est ça, mon ambition. » S’exprimant sur la question de ses adversaires politiques, le nouveau maire a affirmé qu’à Lambanyi, « il n’y a pas d’adversaire politique ». Il a ajouté : « Nous avons montré cela aujourd’hui à travers des élections civilisées, pacifiques et consensuelles. » Mamadou Yaya BARRY L’article Lambanyi : Baba Alimou prend les rênes de la commune et promet « mes ambitions, c’est de construire, de faire de Lambanyi la première référence en Guinée » est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: Sahbi Smara | Condamnation confirmée en appel, la peine légèrement réduite
items.description: La Cour d’appel de Tunis a statué, ce jeudo 2 juillet 2026, sur le cas de l'ancien député Sahbi Smara poursuivi pour plusieurs chefs d'accusation. La Chambre criminelle près la Cour d’appel a ainsi confirmé la condamnation de l'ancien député, tout en réduisant sa peine passant de onze annnées prononcées en première instance, à neuf ans et demi. Pour rappel, Sahbi Smara est notamment poursuivi pour « complot contre l'État et incitation à la violence armée » sachant qu'il a par ailleurs été condamné, en mars dernier, à 6 mois de prison pour l’agression de la présidente du PDL Abir Moussi, et ce, à l'intérieur du parlement lorsqu'ils étaient tous les deux députés. Y. N. L’article Sahbi Smara | Condamnation confirmée en appel, la peine légèrement réduite est apparu en premier sur Kapitalis .

items.title: N’zérékoré : le nouveau maire, Germain Tavaguèz Kolie, promet paix et cohésion après son élection
items.description: Fraîchement élu maire de la commune urbaine de N’zérékoré, Germain Tavaguèz Kolie a livré ses premières impressions en insistant sur la nécessité de bâtir une gouvernance inclusive et apaisée. « C’est un sentiment de satisfaction. N’zérékoré n’est pas une petite ville, c’est une grande commune de type A, avec des enjeux énormes. Il y avait beaucoup de candidats, c’est la première fois qu’ils étaient six en lice pour le poste de maire. Il fallait donc travailler pour que tout le monde se retrouve », a-t-il expliqué. Le nouveau maire a tenu à remercier la GMD pour la confiance placée en lui en le choisissant comme candidat. Après les élections, treize conseillers étaient en compétition, ce qui a rendu nécessaire une alliance. « C’était normal, parce que N’zérékoré avait besoin de ça », a-t-il ajouté. Une équipe représentative de toutes les communautés Selon Germain Tavaguèz Kolie, les vice-maires élus sont issus de toutes les communautés vivant dans la ville. « Ils ont mérité leur poste puisque c’est à la suite d’une élection. Franchement, c’est un bon mélange qui permet à chacun d’avoir son mot à dire pour l’épanouissement de la commune », a-t-il souligné. Il a rappelé que N’zérékoré a longtemps souffert d’injustices malgré ses atouts, notamment sa diversité ethnique et religieuse. Pour lui, la meilleure façon de dépasser les divergences est de travailler ensemble dans la paix. « Les investisseurs nous regardent, il faut donc la paix avant tout », a insisté le maire. Le nouveau locataire de la mairie a également mis en avant l’engagement des candidats pour la paix durant le processus électoral. « Cette équipe composée de personnes diverses va travailler collectivement afin que N’zérékoré connaisse une paix durable. Nous allons mûrir des réflexions autour d’un projet de société commun, choisir les priorités et œuvrer pour le bonheur de la ville et de ses habitants », a-t-il affirmé. Enfin, Germain Tavaguèz Kolie a exprimé sa gratitude envers la population pour la confiance accordée. « Nous allons tout donner pour être à la hauteur des attentes », a-t-il conclu. Amara Souza Soumaoro, correspondant régional à N’zérékoré 621-94-17-77 L’article N’zérékoré : le nouveau maire, Germain Tavaguèz Kolie, promet paix et cohésion après son élection est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: Commune urbaine de Télimélé : Dr Alpha Mohamed Diallo élu maire
items.description: C’est désormais officiel. Dr Alpha Mohamed Diallo, candidat présenté par la Génération pour la modernité et le développement (GMD), a été élu maire de la commune urbaine de Télimélé à l’issue de la cérémonie d’installation et d’élection de l’exécutif communal, tenue ce jeudi 2 juillet 2026 au siège de la mairie. Il est secondé par Mme Maymouna Diallo, Mamadou Bobo Diallo et Idrissa Baldé, qui composent le nouvel exécutif communal. Ancien vice-président de la délégation spéciale et docteur en archéologie, cet homme d’expérience, désormais à la tête de la commune, aura la lourde mission de répondre aux nombreux défis de développement auxquels fait face Télimélé. Entre déficit d’infrastructures scolaires, sanitaires et administratives, besoins en eau potable, énergie et assainissement, ainsi que l’accompagnement des femmes, le nouveau conseil communal devra également s’atteler à la restauration du tissu social fragilisé par endroits, à la régulation de la circulation routière, sans oublier la lutte contre le banditisme et la consommation de stupéfiants, ainsi que l’amélioration des ressources financières de la commune. Le préfet de Télimélé, le colonel Mohamed Camara, a profité de l’occasion pour adresser un message ferme : « Bravo Télimélé. J’ai dit aux maires que je veillerai à leur gestion pour que Télimélé bouge. Il n’y a pas d’amitié : celui qui s’amuse avec les deniers publics aura à faire avec moi », a-t-il martelé.

items.title: Corée du Sud : un Starbucks qui surplombe la Corée du Nord attire les touristes
items.description: Un visiteur tenant un gobelet Starbucks prend un selfie tout en observant le côté nord-coréen depuis une plateforme d’observation sud-coréenne, à l’Aegibong Peace Ecopark de Gimpo, le 1er juillet 2026. Le contraste ne pourrait être plus saisissant : des personnes sirotant un café Starbucks — symbole de la mondialisation et du capitalisme — tout en contemplant la nation la plus fermée au monde, la Corée du Nord communiste. JUNG YEON-JE / AFP

items.title: Burkina : Clarification des horaires des Journées de travail continu
items.description: L'hebdomadaire Conseil des ministres s'est tenu ce jeudi, sous la présidence du Camarade Capitaine Ibrahim TRAORÉ, Président du Faso, Chef de l'État. Selon le Ministre Porte-parole du Gouvernement, le Camarade Pingdwendé Gilbert OUÉDRAOGO, le Conseil a examiné plusieurs dossiers et adopté d'importantes décisions pour la bonne marche de la Nation et pour le plein épanouissement des Burkinabè, conformément à la vision de la Révolution Progressiste Populaire. Pour le compte du ministère des Serviteurs du Peuple, le Conseil a adopté deux décrets, portant respectivement, Horaires de travail dans les administrations du secteur public et Conditions et modalités d'instauration de la Journée de travail continu dans les administrations du secteur public et privé au Burkina Faso. Selon le Ministre des Serviteurs du Peuple, le Camarade Mathias TRAORÉ, « le gouvernement a procédé à la relecture des textes de 2015 et 2017 afin de corriger des lacunes réglementaires constatées dans l'application de la Journée de travail continu ». Le premier décret clarifie les horaires de travail dans l'Administration publique sans les modifier. Ces horaires sont fixés de 7h30 à 12h30 et de 13h00 à 16h00 du lundi au jeudi, et de 7h30 à 12h30 puis de 13h30 à 16h30 le vendredi. Le second décret dispose que la Journée de travail continu, liée à certains évènements, qui visait le privé, s'applique désormais clairement aux deux secteurs (public et privé), fixant cette journée de 7h30 à 14h. Minute.bf

items.title: Sonfonia : Fodé Imourana Traoré et Mohamed Lamine Sylla prennent respectivement les postes de maire et vice-maire
items.description: La commune de Sonfonia tient désormais son exécutif communal. À l’issue du vote des 45 conseillers communaux, organisé ce jeudi 2 juillet 2026, Fodé Imourana Traoré a été élu maire avec 29 voix . Issu de la liste GMD (Génération pour la modernité et le développement) , le nouveau maire dirigera la commune aux côtés de sept vice-maires , parmi lesquels Mohamed Lamine Sylla , du mouvement Sonfonia Fé , élu vice-maire, ainsi que Safiatou Camara , seule femme de l’exécutif communal, portée au poste de 5ᵉ vice-maire. À l’issue de son élection, Fodé Imourana Traoré a exprimé sa gratitude envers les populations de Sonfonia et les conseillers communaux qui lui ont accordé leur confiance. «Nous remercions Dieu Tout-Puissant ainsi que les populations de Sonfonia qui nous ont fait confiance. Les 45 conseillers communaux, à leur tour, nous ont confié la responsabilité de diriger cette commune. Aujourd’hui, Sonfonia a gagné. Les populations ont fait preuve de maturité en votant dans le calme et la transparence, tout comme les conseillers communaux qui ont démontré un grand sens de responsabilité. Cela nous oblige à préserver cet esprit de paix et de transparence. Nous demandons à Dieu de guider nos pas afin que nous puissions accomplir avec succès la mission que les citoyens nous ont confiée », a-t-il déclaré. Le nouveau conseil communal est investi pour un mandat de cinq ans . Poursuivant son intervention, le maire a insisté sur la nécessité de privilégier l’unité et la gouvernance inclusive: «Nous sommes engagés à travailler dans la paix, la transparence et l’inclusivité. Aujourd’hui, il n’y a ni gagnant ni perdant. C’est Sonfonia qui a gagné. Les différentes listes ont fait preuve d’un grand esprit de loyauté tout au long du processus électoral. Cela montre que nous pouvons compter les uns sur les autres, ce qui est essentiel pour le bon fonctionnement d’un conseil communal. » De son côté, Safiatou Camara , unique femme élue au sein de l’exécutif communal, s’est engagée à répondre aux attentes des habitants de Sonfonia et à contribuer pleinement au développement de la commune. Mayi Cissé L’article Sonfonia : Fodé Imourana Traoré et Mohamed Lamine Sylla prennent respectivement les postes de maire et vice-maire est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .
